stories.name: weights-token-required-error
stories.title: Add TokenRequiredError and update download_flux_weights() return type
stories.description: Extend textbrush/model/weights.py with two changes. First, define TokenRequiredError as a new Exception subclass at module level. Second, update download_flux_weights() to (a) return Path(snapshot_download(...)) on success instead of None, and (b) raise TokenRequiredError (not RuntimeError) when snapshot_download raises an exception whose message contains '401' or '403'. All other exceptions still produce RuntimeError. Update ensure_flux_available() error message to reference '--download-model' instead of 'make download-model' and 'scripts/download_model.py'. Add unit tests in tests/test_weights.py covering: TokenRequiredError is Exception subclass; download_flux_weights returns Path on success; TokenRequiredError raised on 401 and 403 errors; ensure_flux_available error message contains '--download-model'. Check linting after implementation.

includes: download_flux_weights() return type changed from None to Path
download_flux_weights() raises TokenRequiredError on 401/403 auth failures
ensure_flux_available() error message updated to reference --download-model
excludes: CLI argument parsing or dispatch logic
Token resolution from HF_TOKEN env var or config (HF hub library handles HF_TOKEN natively; config token pass-through is handled in Story 02)

test_fixtures.description: Patch of textbrush.model.weights.snapshot_download returning a str path for successful download tests
test_fixtures.reason: Avoids real HuggingFace network call in unit tests — permanent test infrastructure

test_fixtures.description: Patch of textbrush.model.weights.is_flux_available returning False to force ensure_flux_available() to raise
test_fixtures.reason: Avoids real HF cache check in unit tests — permanent test infrastructure

scenarios.name: token-required-error-importable
scenarios.description: Verify TokenRequiredError can be imported and is an Exception subclass
user_steps: Run: python -c 'from textbrush.model.weights import TokenRequiredError; raise TokenRequiredError("test")'
Observe: process exits with unhandled TokenRequiredError (not ImportError)
assertions: Exit code non-zero due to unhandled exception (not ImportError or AttributeError)

scenarios.name: ensure-flux-available-references-download-flag
user_steps: Run: python -c 'from textbrush.model.weights import ensure_flux_available; ensure_flux_available()' with model absent from cache

stories.notes: No dependency on Stories 02 or 03. The token flow for config.huggingface.token is resolved in Story 02 where the CLI passes the token to download_flux_weights(). This story only changes the weights module exception hierarchy and return type.

stories.name: cli-download-model-flag
stories.title: Add --download-model flag to CLI with full dispatch and token resolution
stories.description: Extend textbrush/cli.py with the following changes: (1) In build_parser(), add '--download-model' as action='store_true', default=False; change '--prompt' to required=False. (2) In validate_args(), add parser.error() calls for: neither --prompt nor --download-model present; --download-model combined with --prompt; --download-model combined with --headless. Note: validate_args currently raises ValueError, but the spec mandates parser.error() for these mutual-exclusivity checks — use parser.error() (requires passing parser into validate_args or calling parser.error via a stored reference). (3) In main(), before the headless dispatch block, add: if args.download_model — print license notice to stderr; print 'Downloading FLUX.1 schnell model (~23 GB)...' to stderr; import download_flux_weights and TokenRequiredError from textbrush.model.weights; resolve token from HF_TOKEN env var, then config.huggingface.token, and pass token to download_flux_weights if found; catch TokenRequiredError and print multi-line instructions including HF_TOKEN and https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens to stderr, exit 1; catch generic Exception and print 'Download failed: <msg>' to stderr, exit 1; on success print 'Model downloaded successfully to: <path>' to stderr, exit 0. Add unit tests in tests/test_cli.py for all validation paths and dispatch outcomes. Check linting after implementation.

includes: validate_args(): mutual exclusivity checks for --download-model vs --prompt, vs --headless, and neither flag present
main(): license notice printed to stderr before download
main(): 'Downloading FLUX.1 schnell model (~23 GB)...' progress message to stderr
main(): token resolution from HF_TOKEN env var, then config.huggingface.token
main(): TokenRequiredError caught with multi-line instructions to stderr including HF_TOKEN and token URL
main(): generic Exception caught with 'Download failed:' message to stderr
main(): success path prints path to stderr and exits 0
excludes: download_flux_weights() return type or exception change (Story 01 owns this)
README or documentation changes (Story 03 owns this)

mock_dependencies.reason: Story 01 implements these; unit tests in this story patch them at the CLI import boundary

scenarios.name: no-args-error
scenarios.description: Verify invoking textbrush with no arguments produces actionable error

stories.notes: validate_args() currently raises ValueError for semantic errors. The spec mandates parser.error() for the three new mutual-exclusivity checks (which produces exit code 2 and is argparse-standard). The implementer must reconcile this: either pass the parser object into validate_args(), or add a separate early-check step in main() before validate_args(). Choose the approach that minimally disturbs existing ValueError behaviour for other validations.

stories.name: documentation-updates
stories.title: Update README with --download-model example and correct token env var
stories.description: Update README.md to replace the model download setup instructions: replace 'export HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN=...' with 'export HF_TOKEN=...' (canonical name used by HF hub library and the new CLI feature); replace 'make download-model' with 'uv run textbrush --download-model'. Verify the README download section no longer references HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN or make download-model as the primary download path. No code changes; no new test files needed for this story. Verify by grepping README.md after the change.

includes: README.md: HF_TOKEN as the env var (replacing HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN)
excludes: Any changes to textbrush/cli.py or textbrush/model/weights.py
user-stories.md (mentioned in spec but not an acceptance criterion for this epic)
docs/troubleshooting.md (mentioned in spec but not a verifiable acceptance criterion here)
specs/spec.md cross-reference note (internal documentation, not user-facing)

scenarios.name: readme-download-command-updated
scenarios.description: Verify README.md references uv run textbrush --download-model and HF_TOKEN
assertions: 'HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN' absent or not the primary recommended env var

stories.notes: This story is independent of Stories 01 and 02 from a code dependency standpoint — README.md changes do not depend on the Python implementation being complete. However, the correct token env var name (HF_TOKEN) and the flag name (--download-model) are specified in the spec and acceptance criteria, so Story 03 can proceed in parallel with Stories 01 and 02.

seams.data: TokenRequiredError class and download_flux_weights() returning Path

destination.artifact: textbrush.cli.main() download dispatch block importing TokenRequiredError and calling download_flux_weights()

dependency_notes: Story 02 imports TokenRequiredError and calls download_flux_weights() defined in Story 01. At unit-test time this dependency is mocked via patch at the CLI import boundary (MOCK-02-001), so Story 02 tests are fully independent. At runtime both stories must be complete. Story 03 has no code dependency on Stories 01 or 02.
mock_resolution_plan: MOCK-02-001 (download_flux_weights and TokenRequiredError) is a unit-test patch, not a production stub. Story 01 delivers the real implementation. Story 02 tests use the patch pattern 'patch("textbrush.cli.download_flux_weights")' per project conventions (patch at the module where used, not where defined). No temporary production mocks are introduced; all mocks listed are permanent test fixtures or test-boundary patches.
